summary: Developed additional camera and debug-camera code, primarily by adding several camera subject/list-management and movement-smoothing routines in src/melee/cm/camera.c, expanding camera structs in src/melee/cm/types.h, adding a CameraUnkGlobals forward declaration, and replacing several db_2253 debug-camera UNK_RET prototypes with typed HSD_GObj/Vec3/f32 signatures plus implemented C-stick camera control helpers. Review evidence is minimal; the only human comment was a brief approval-style 'Nice work, thanks!'.
what_changed: Added extern CameraBox list-head/list-tail/free-list globals cm_804D6458, cm_804D6460, and cm_804D6468 to camera.c.

Implemented Camera_80028F5C to initialize/reset a CameraBox subject, including id/state field x8, zeroed Vec3 fields, bit clears on xC_b0, default extents at x2C/x34, copied current extents into x40/x48, and zeroed x54/x60.

Implemented Camera_80029044 to allocate a CameraBox from cm_804D6458, move it into the active list tracked by cm_804D6460/cm_804D6468, report and infinite-loop on allocation failure, and reset it through Camera_80028F5C.
Implemented Camera_800290D4 to remove a CameraBox from the active doubly-linked list and push it back onto the free list.

Added static CameraUnkGlobals cm_803BCCA0 and implemented Camera_80029AAC to interpolate CameraMovement interest toward target_interest using CameraBounds span, global camera timing cm_80452C68.unk_2bc, and threshold/speed fields in cm_803BCCA0.
Changed CameraBox layout in types.h from anonymous filler to explicit next/prev pointers and added Vec3 fields at x54 and x60.
Changed CameraBounds.subjects from float to int, matching its use as a count/presence check in Camera_80029AAC.
Added struct CameraUnkGlobals with float padding x0[11] and named float fields x2C, x30, x34, and x38.
In db_2253.h, added baselib cobj/gobj includes and typed prototypes for fn_80227904, fn_802279E8, fn_80227B64, fn_80227CAC, and fn_80227FE0 instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Implemented fn_80227904 to read HSD_PadMasterStatus C-stick values, apply a 0.2 deadzone, dispatch to camera rotate/zoom/pan helpers depending on button bits, and reset lbl_804D6B5C to 0x3C.
Implemented fn_80227B64 as a thin wrapper that calls fn_802279E8 with camera position/interest Vec3 slots inside cm_80453004 when C-stick input is nonzero.
Implemented fn_80227CAC and fn_80227FE0 using HSD_CObj eye/left/up vectors, eye distance, FOV, tanf, and PSVEC operations to adjust debug-camera interest/position data stored in cm_80453004.
smart_moves: Moved away from opaque filler and UNK prototypes only where the diff had concrete usage evidence: CameraBox next/prev pointers are justified by list manipulation, CameraBounds.subjects is treated as an integer presence/count, and db_2253 functions now expose HSD_GObj, Vec3, and float parameter intent.
Kept partially understood global camera data as CameraUnkGlobals with offset-style field names rather than over-naming semantics not yet proven.
Used existing HSD_CObj vector accessors and Dolphin PSVEC math APIs for camera movement, matching the domain behavior instead of hand-rolling vector arithmetic.
Factored C-stick input handling into dispatcher and helper routines: fn_80227904 normalizes/deadzones input, while fn_80227B64/fn_80227CAC/fn_80227FE0 handle movement modes.
Preserved address-based symbol naming for unknown functions/globals while improving type signatures, which keeps symbols searchable and stable for later decomp passes.
decomp_lessons: When a struct filler region starts being manipulated as links, replace filler with explicit next/prev pointers and let list algorithms validate the offsets.
A field used only as a zero/nonzero subject count should not remain float just because surrounding bounds fields are floats; update the type when usage proves integer semantics.
Offset-style temporary names such as x2C/x30/x34/x38 are preferable for globals with unclear semantics; they allow matching progress without inventing names.

For camera code, HSD_CObjGetEyeVector, HSD_CObjGetLeftVector, HSD_CObjGetUpVector, HSD_CObjGetEyeDistance, HSD_CObjGetFov, PSVECScale, PSVECAdd, and PSVECSubtract are strong clues for debug-camera position/interest adjustment routines.
Deadzone checks on HSD_PadMasterStatus normalized substick axes can reveal controller-driven camera helpers and their parameter types.
Free-list and active-list camera subject management can be reconstructed from updates to global head/tail pointers and per-node next/prev fields.
naming_conventions: Kept undecompiled or partially understood functions in address form, e.g. Camera_80028F5C, Camera_80029044, Camera_800290D4, Camera_80029AAC, fn_80227904, fn_80227B64, fn_80227CAC, and fn_80227FE0.
Used subsystem prefixes matching existing files: Camera_ for camera module functions and fn_ for still-unknown db_2253 routines.
Used offset-style struct field names such as x8, xC_b0, x2C, x34, x54, x60, x2C/x30/x34/x38 where semantics were not confirmed.
Introduced descriptive type names CameraBox, CameraMovement, CameraBounds, and CameraUnkGlobals while retaining conservative field names.
Parameter names in db_2253.c were made behavior-oriented where evident, such as camera, port, cstick_x, and cstick_y.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit zeroing and chained vector assignments in Camera_80028F5C to reproduce initialization order and struct stores.
Used bitwise clears on xC_b0 for individual flags 0x80, 0x40, and 0x20, preserving low-level flag behavior rather than abstracting into named flags.
Modeled allocation failure in Camera_80029044 with OSReport followed by an intentional infinite loop label, matching a crash/assert-style original control flow.
Interpolated movement->interest toward movement->target_interest with clamped factor bounds 1.0f and 0.0001f, preserving float constants and branch behavior.
Used C-stick absolute-value deadzone logic manually instead of fabsf, likely to match generated code shape.
Kept casts into cm_80453004 as Vec3 pointer arithmetic for unknown global layout, allowing matching without prematurely defining the whole struct.
Included MSL/trigf.h and used tanf with the literal degree-to-radian factor 0.017453292f for FOV-based debug-camera pan scaling.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename CameraBox fields x54/x60 or CameraUnkGlobals fields x2C/x30/x34/x38.
Search for subsequent work defining the full type/layout of cm_80453004 instead of Vec3 pointer casts.
Check whether fn_80227CAC condition was later corrected or clarified, since the diff shows a suspicious duplicated temp_cr0_eq check around cstick_y == 0.0f.
Look for later camera.c matching improvements around Camera_80028F5C initialization comments for bounds/default_size.
Search for later replacement of cm_804D6458/cm_804D6460/cm_804D6468 with descriptive free-list/active-list names.
